FLASH FLOOD REPORTED IN CATHEDRAL VALLEY, THE NORTHERN PORTION OF CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK. ROADS IN THE AREA WERE WASHED OUT, STRANDING VISITORS. PARK RANGERS REPORTED THAT THE CATHEDRAL VALLEY ROAD HAD A 2 1/2 FOOT DEEP TRENCH CREATED BY THE WATER.
FLASH FLOOD IN WAHWEAP CREEK. DAMAGE SUSTAINED ALONG THE NIPPLE CREEK AND SMOKEY MOUNTAIN ROADS.
DEBRIS FLOW ORIGINATING IN THE WOOD HOLLOW BURN SCAR CROSSED SR 132 AND IMPACTED STRUCTURES WEST OF THE TOWN OF FOUNTAIN GREEN.
FLASH FLOODING ON THE SEELEY BURN SCAR. SOUTH HUGHES, NUCKWOODWARD, AND CHUTE CANYON ALL EXPERIENCED FLASH FLOODING.
DEBRIS FLOW ON SEELEY BURN SCAR AFFECTING SOUTH HUGHES CANYON. THE DEBRIS FLOW WENT ACROSS HIGHWAY 31.
3 FT FLOOD WAVE IN SULPHUR CREEK.
SIGNIFICANT FLASH FLOODING IN ZION NP.
HIGHWAY 143 CLOSED NEAR BRIAN HEAD DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
WATER FROM THE ESCALANTE RIVER FLOWING OVER HIGHWAY 12.
FLASH FLOODING ALONG THE SKUTUMPAH ROAD.
FLASH FLOODING ON KANAB CREEK AND OAK CREEK.
DEBRIS FLOW FROM THE SEELEY BURN SCAR CROSSED HIGHWAY 31. A BRIDGE WAS LOST ON HIGHWAY 31.

LARGE DEBRIS FLOWS OCCURRED ON THE SEELEY BURN SCAR. HIGHWAY 31 WAS UNDERCUT IN A FEW PLACES BY HUNTINGTON CREEK AND DEBRIS OF UP TO 3 FEET SPANNED NEARLY A MILE. HIGHWAY 31 THROUGH HUNTINGTON CANYON IS EXPECTED TO BE CLOSED FOR SOME TIME AS IT IS IMPASSABLE. THERE WAS ALSO A 5 FOOT WALL OF WATER IN POLE CANYON AND A LOGJAM IN HUNTINGTON CREEK. A LOADER VEHICLE WAS PUSHED INTO A DITCH.
THE ROAD TO GRAND WASH IN CAPITOL REEF IS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.
THE SCENIC DRIVE IN CAPITOL REEF IS CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
*** 1 INJ *** TREE KNOCKED DOWN AT LIBERTY WELLS SENIOR CENTER, WITH THE TREE FALLING ONTO THREE SENIOR CITIZENS.
MILFORD MUNICIPAL ASOS.
ROCKS WASHED ONTO HIGHWAY 14 EAST OF CEDAR CITY CLOSED THE HIGHWAY.
FLASH FLOODING REPORTED IN SPRY CANYON. ROCKS ALSO REPORTED ON THE MAIN ROAD RESULTING FROM HEAVY RAINFALL.
FLASH FLOODING REPORTED AT THE CONFLUENCE OF COTTONWOOD CREEK AND THE PARIA RIVER
FLASH FLOODING IN PINE CREEK. NUMEROUS ROCKS ALSO WASHED ON TO HIGHWAY 9 BETWEEN THE SWITCHBACKS AND THE TUNNEL AFFECTING TRAFFIC.
SEVERAL REPORTS OF FLOODING IN HILDALE
SIGNIFICANT FLASH FLOODING IN KEYHOLE AND PINE CREEK CANYONS.
LARGE FLASH FLOOD ALONG THE PARIA RIVER. SPOTTER ESTIMATED FIRST 200 TO 300 YARDS OF FLOOD WAS COMPOSED OF DEBRIS INCLUDING TREES AND LOGS.
SIGNIFICANT FLASH FLOODING ALONG NORTH CREEK INCLUDING THE SUBWAY. FLASH FLOODING ALSO REPORTED IN SPRY CANYON AND ALONG THE NORTH FORK OF THE VIRGIN.
FLASH FLOOD REPORTED OVERNIGHT ALONG WAHWEAP CREEK, DAMAGING THE SMOKEY MOUNTAIN ROAD.
TORNADO REPORTED BY COUNTY SHERIFF ALONG THE BEAVER/LINCOLN COUNTY LINE.
FLASH FLOODING IN CAPITOL REEF NP INCLUDING SULPHUR CREEK.
BUCKSKIN GULCH FLASH FLOODED AT 3PM.
LARGE FLASH FLOOD IN THE BUCKSKIN GULCH AREA NEAR SR89.
MAJOR FLASH FLOODING IN COYOTE CREEK, TRIBUTARY OF WAWHEAP DRAINAGE NEAR BIGWATER. LARGE TREES AND DEBRIS ON FRONT OF WAVE.

MULTIPLE ROAD CLOSURES DUE TO FLASH FLOODING ON THURSDAY...AUG. 23RD INCLUDING SMOKEY MT. ROAD...NIPPLE CREEK RD IN THE WAHWEAP DRAINAGE...HOUSEROCK RD IN THE BUCKSKIN DRAINAGE...AND COTTONWOOD RD NEAR KODACHROME BASIN STATE PARK.
FLASH FLOOD ALONG BUCKSKIN GULCH WASHED OUT HOUSE ROCK VALLEY ROAD, MAKING IT IMPOSSIBLE TO REACH THE WAVE, A VERY POPULAR HIKE IN THE AREA.
FLASH FLOOD IN JOHNSON CANYON. PARK RANGER WHO LIVES UP JOHNSON CANYON CALLED IT AVERAGE SIZE.

FLASH FLOODING REPORTED ALONG WAHWEAP CREEK. THIS WAS A LARGER FLOOD THAN THOSE SEEN IN RECENT WEEKS, AND LEFT A 5 FOOT DEEP TRENCH IN THE WASH. SPOTTER ESTIMATES THAT THE FLOOD WAS 50 TO 60 FEET WIDE.

NUMEROUS HOUSES FLOODED IN SARAGOGA SPRINGS AFTER HEAVY RAINFALL CAUSED A RETENTION POND TO FAIL. HIGHWAY 68 ALSO CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.
NATIONAL PARK RANGERS REPORTED A FLASH FLOOD IN SULPHUR CREEK.
FREMONT JUNCTION - 6801 FT
US-40 @ STARVATION - 5720 FT
FLOODING REPORTED OVER THE BRIDGE WHERE OLD US HIGHWAY 91 CROSSES THE SANTA CLARA RIVER. THIS FLOODING CAUSED THE ROAD TO BE CLOSED FOR A FEW HOURS THROUGH THE LATE MORNING AND EARLY AFTERNOON.
THE BEAVER DAM WASH EXPERIENCED A FLASH FLOOD, WITH THE READING ON THE GAUGE INCREASING FROM 96 CFS TO 4367 CFS IN ONE HOUR.
FLASH FLOODING REPORTED ON STOUT CREEK. THIS FLOODING DAMAGED A FLY FISHING OPERATION ALONG THE CREEK.

FLOODING REPORTED IN ZION NATIONAL PARK, INCLUDING IN THE NARROWS. THE NORTH FORK OF THE VIRGIN RIVER ROSE FROM 52 CFS TO 1004 CFS IN ONE HOUR. LARGE WATERFALLS COMING OFF CLIFFS CLOSED NUMEROUS HIKES, TEMPORARILY STRANDING HIKERS.

THE LAUB DIKE IN SANTA CLARA FAILED FOLLOWING HEAVY RAINFALL, RELEASING ALL OF THE WATER THAT HAD ACCUMULATED IN THE ASSOCIATED RETENTION POND. THIS QUICKLY CAUSED FLOODING IN PARTS OF SANTA CLARA, WITH SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE REPORTED TO BOTH BUSINESS AND RESIDENTIAL AREAS.
FLASH FLOODING REPORTED IN CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK. WATER WAS FLOWING ACROSS THE SCENIC DRIVE, CAUSING PARK OFFICIALS TO CLOSE THE ROAD.
FLASH FLOODING WAS REPORTED ON SULPHUR CREEK IN CAPITOL REEF NATIONAL PARK.
SHERWOOD HILLS - 5039 FT
